title: Phillyrea brachiata
intro: Phillyrea brachiata, also known as the common privet or sea privet, is a small to medium-sized evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, offering health benefits such as wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Phillyrea brachiata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Phillyrea brachiata is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, skin problems, and infections.
taxonomy: Phillyrea brachiata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae. It is a member of the order Lamiales and is native to the Mediterranean region. The genus Phillyrea is a small group of evergreen shrubs and small trees. Phillyrea brachiata is also known as the Greek privet or sea olive. It is a relatively small shrub or small tree with small, fragrant white flowers.
